Today, the Trump Administration is proud to announce the United States of America’s updated Childhood Vaccination Schedule. This Schedule is rooted in the Gold Standard of Science, and widely agreed upon by Scientists and Experts all over the World. Effective today, America will no longer require 72 “jabs” for our beautiful, healthy children. We are moving to a far more reasonable Schedule, where all children will only be recommended to receive Vaccinations for 11 of the most serious and dangerous diseases. Parents can still choose to give their children all of the Vaccinations, if they wish, and they will still be covered by insurance. However, this updated Schedule finally aligns the United States with other Developed Nations around the World. Congratulations to HHS Secretary Bobby Kennedy, CDC Acting Director Jim O’Neil, FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, CMS Administrator Dr. Oz, NIH Director Jay Bhattacharya, and all of the Medical Experts and Professionals who worked very hard to make this happen. Many Americans, especially the “MAHA Moms,” have been praying for these COMMON SENSE reforms for many years. Thank you for your attention to this matter!\nDONALD J. I am pleased to announce the nomination of Andrew Davis to serve as Judge on the United States District Court for the Western District of Texas. A proud graduate of Columbia Law School and Rice University, Andrew brings tremendous experience to his new role, after delivering strong results as Chief Counsel to Senator Ted Cruz, Assistant Solicitor General of Texas, and former Law Clerk to Judges Reena Raggi and Sidney Fitzwater. As Judge, Andrew will uphold the Rule of Law, and protect the Constitutional Rights of the Great People of Texas. Congratulations Andrew!

After graduating from Baylor University and Baylor Law School, Chris spent nearly 20 years as a Prosecutor before becoming a Judge on the 213th State Judicial Court. In his new role, Chris will continue to be a fearless Champion for the incredible people of Texas. He will uphold the Constitution, ensure Law and Order, and keep our Citizens safe. Congratulations!

I am pleased to announce the nomination of John Shepherd to serve as Judge to the United States District Court for the Western District of Arkansas! John is currently a Judge on the 13th State Judicial District in El Dorado, Arkansas, after previously working as a Prosecuting Attorney for the District. He is TOUGH and SMART, graduating magna cum laude from the University of Arkansas School of Law, with a Bachelor’s Degree from Rice University, where he was a terrific football player! John brings a wealth of experience to his new role. He will be a bold Advocate for Law and Order, and a tireless Champion of our Great Constitution. Congratulations John!

I am pleased to announce the nomination of Daniel Traynor to serve as Judge on the United States Court of Appeals for the Eighth Circuit in North Dakota. During his time as a Federal District Court Judge, Daniel has issued courageous decisions, and blocked illegal Federal Government actions under the previous Administration. Daniel received his Undergraduate and Law Degrees from the University of North Dakota and, before becoming a Judge, was an incredible attorney, and Law Clerk to Chief Justice Gerald W. VandeWalle of the North Dakota Supreme Court. The Citizens of the Eighth Circuit will have a true Constitutional Champion with Daniel on the Bench. Congratulations Daniel!

I am pleased to announce the nomination of Daniel Domenico to serve as Judge on the United States Court of Appeals for the Tenth Circuit in Colorado. As a Judge on the Federal District Court in Colorado, Dan has displayed an exemplary record of protecting Citizens’ Constitutional Rights. Before becoming a Judge, Dan spent a decade as Colorado’s Solicitor General where he defended Colorado’s Taxpayer Bill of Rights, and challenged Radical Government Overreach. A proud graduate of Georgetown University and University of Virginia School of Law, Dan was the first Law Clerk of the Great Judge Timothy Tymkovich, and is now being nominated to succeed him on the Court. The wonderful people of Colorado can have great confidence that Dan will deliver strong results, defend the Constitution, and uphold the Rule of Law on the Tenth Circuit. Congratulations Dan!

I am pleased to nominate Kasdin Mitchell to serve as Judge on the United States District Court for the Northern District of Texas. Kasdin received her Bachelor’s Degree from Yale University, and her Juris Doctor from Yale Law School, one of the top Law Schools in the Country! After clerking for both Justice Clarence Thomas and Judge William Pryor, Kasdin is now a partner at Kirkland and Ellis in Dallas, Texas, and has a strong record of fiercely defending the Second Amendment and Religious Liberty, which are always under siege by the Radical Left. I have no doubt that Kasdin will be a Powerful Defender of the Rule of Law in the GREAT State of Texas. Congratulations Kasdin!

I am proud to announce the nomination of Angela Colmenero to serve as Judge on the United States District Court for the Southern District of Texas. Angela has done an incredible job as Deputy Chief of Staff to Governor Greg Abbott and, previously, as his Principal Deputy General Counsel. Before joining the Governor’s Office, Angela worked as an attorney, and Senior Official in the Office of the Texas Attorney General. A proud graduate of Notre Dame Law School and the University of Texas, Angela clerked for Judge A. Joe Fish, and is well prepared to serve the wonderful people of Texas. She will do a tremendous job, and always protect our Great Constitution. Congratulations Angela!

I am pleased to announce the nomination of Antonio Pozos to serve as Judge on the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Pennsylvania. A partner at a major Law Firm in Philadelphia, Tony brings great experience to his new role, previously serving as a Federal Prosecutor handling major fraud and corruption cases. Tony clerked for Judge Anthony J. Trenga, and received his J.D. from the University of Michigan Law School, and B.A. from Harvard College. Tony will be a fantastic Federal Judge for the Great People of Pennsylvania. Congratulations Tony!

It is my Great Honor to nominate Michael Martin to serve as Judge on the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Michigan! Mike has served as a Federal Prosecutor for more than two decades, primarily as an Assistant U.S. Attorney for the Eastern District of Michigan. He is the Chief of the Office’s Criminal Division, and former Chief of its National Security Unit. Receiving his J.D. from Boston College Law School, M.A. in National Security Studies from Georgetown University, and B.A. from Miami University, Mike also clerked for the late Judges Hugh Bownes and Edward F. Harrington. The people of Michigan are truly fortunate that a great public servant like Mike is joining the Federal Bench. Congratulations Mike!
